issues.title: Non-standard derivative equation with additional boundary condition
issues.detail: The derivative equation for h includes an if-condition (if (h <= 0 and Q_net < 0) then 0 else (Q_net / A_tank)), which is not explicitly required by the original user task and may introduce unnecessary complexity. The user task only mandates non-negative boundary protection in sqrt calculations (using max(h,0)), which is already implemented. This additional logic could potentially lead to confusion or non-physical behavior in generalized cases, though for the specific parameters (Q_in=0.0), it does not affect simulation results. It is recommended to simplify the derivative equation to der(h) = Q_net / A_tank for clarity, relying solely on max(h,0) for protection as specified.
issues.evidence: In model_code, equation: der(h) = if (h <= 0 and Q_net < 0) then 0 else (Q_net / A_tank);

recommendations: Simplify the derivative equation to der(h) = Q_net / A_tank to align with standard mass conservation, since non-negative protection is already handled via max(h,0) in outflow calculations.
Consider removing the discrete event 'when h <= 0 then reinit(h, 0);' if the derivative equation is simplified, as max(h,0) should suffice for numerical noise protection.
